results.possibleIdentity: David E. Goodwin (1923–2015) was a professional electrical engineer who spent a significant portion of his career at M&T Chemicals Inc. (Metal & Thermit Corporation). Based in Matawan and Rahway, New Jersey, he resided in Middletown, NJ, from 1959 until his death. During the late 1960s and early 1970s, he co-authored several technical patents assigned to M&T Chemicals, primarily focusing on electrical power systems, solid-state rectifiers, and control circuitry for electroplating and galvanizing plants. Notable inventions include a control circuit for plural section rectifiers and systems for compensating reactive current in networks, often developed in collaboration with fellow engineer James V. McNulty. His professional profile as an electrical engineer at M&T Chemicals in New Jersey aligns with the records of the technical service organization Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), which maintained a database of engineers interested in collaborating with artists during that period. In his personal life, Goodwin was a World War II Army veteran who served in the 84th Infantry during the Battle of the Bulge and later remained active in antique radio and computer clubs.
